public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as ward mediator.
appearance: Bright-plumed road gear, polished fittings, and visible attention to presentation mark the role before the voice does.
manner: Projects readiness without theatricality, as if discipline matters more than spectacle. In Panim, that reads as someone grave, measured, and ritual-aware, with constant awareness of consequence, grief, and divine mediation, and the habits of a ward mediator still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of ward mediator in and around Panim Haven, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Panim Haven workable through the obligations of ward mediator while managing the immediate pressure of divine balance obligations in Panim.
hidden_pressure: Must prepare for violence without letting the surrounding institution look openly unstable. Around Panim Haven, that usually means carrying divine balance obligations, sacrificial and mediation decorum, institutional scrutiny without letting the role of ward mediator visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Celis Lanthorn can pull the party into a dispute over readiness, chain of command, or blamed failure near Panim Haven.
Celis Lanthorn will trade useful tactical knowledge if the party solves a security problem too sensitive for ordinary channels.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained. Appearance extended under V33_1 from role/locality/public-signal derivation rather than decorative fabrication.
public_signal: Weapon discipline, drilled stillness, and practiced readiness mark Celis Lanthorn as someone tied to organized force rather than casual violence. In Panim Haven, this lands inside a public field already shaped by House Panim.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Panim Haven: drill yards, fortifications, command posts, or exposed routes

first_impression: Celis Lanthorn presents as Projects readiness without theatricality, as if discipline matters more than spectacle. In Panim, that reads as someone grave, measured, and ritual-aware, with constant awareness of consequence, grief, and divine mediation, and the habits of a ward mediator still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Ward Mediator, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in Principality of Panim.
what_they_refuse: Celis Lanthorn refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing Ward Mediator too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Celis Lanthorn can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Celis Lanthorn adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Celis Lanthorn is Known locally through day-to-day work as ward mediator. and rarely acts without a second motive.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Ward Mediator.
